def: = اکهڙنا ukhaṛnā, q.v. v.t. To root up, eradicate, extirpate, pluck up or out, tear up or off, dig up; to break off, disjoin, sunder; to dislocate, displace; to take out, extract, draw out; to detach, separate, remove; to set (one) against, estrange, alienate; entice away, win over (from), buy off; to drive away, put away, do away with, dismiss; to upset, overturn, subvert; to scatter, disperse; to demolish, desolate, destroy, lay waste, devastate, exterminate
s.f. Holding aloof (from), avoiding, shunning; retirement, retreat.

adj. S. उत् out, चट् to break off, H. चट् the sound made by the breaking of any thing brittle. 1. Separated; broken off. 2. Alienated; estranged. Yahāṅ se jī uchāṭ ho gayā . 3. Displeased; dissatisfied; weary. Sailānī jīvṛā uchāṭ hai . The roving mind is never content. 4. Restless; wild; deranged; crazy. Arī sakhī, tū kahāṅ rahī, ham sab dekheṅ bāṭ, Kahāṅ phire baurī huī, kahāṅ lagī ūchāt . Song. Chaubolā . Where have you been, my dear! We all have looked for you, Where have you madly roamed, what makes you look so wild? 5. Down-hearted; downcast; dull ( udās ). uchāṭ , n. f. Disgust; a very strong desire to be separated. uchāṭ ho'nā,—khānā , v. n. To be alienated; estranged; etc. Phir nahīṅ lagtā hai jī, jis jā se jī khāve uchāṭ .
s. f. ( उत् and r. चट Break, pierce, injure) The act of vexing or rendering sorrowful, vexation.
v. a. See uchṭā'nā .

v. n. 1. To be separated, detached, broken, disjoined; to come off; fall off (as plaster from a wall); go off; to be dropped, or lost. 2. To rebound (as a sword striking a hard body obliquely); to recoil; glance off; fly, or spring back; ricochet. Golī patthar pe lag-ke uchaṭ gaī . 3. To spring; bound; leap. 4. To slip through one's fingers; fly; flee away; to be scared; to scamper. Bandūq kī āwāz se shikār uchat gayā . 5. To be alienated, estranged, displeased, weary of, turned from. 1. Dil uchṭe pe koī kām nahīṅ hotā . 2. Tumhārī mār pīṭ se ranḍī uchaṭ gaī . 6. To be hurt, offended, annoyed, displeased; to take amiss, or ill. 7. To be tired, disgusted, sick of. Paṛhne se dil uchaṭ gayā. uchaṭtī sī , adv. Slightly; in a slight degree. Uchaṭtī sī choṭ lagī hai. nīṅd uchaṭnā , v. n. To have one's sleep broken or disturbed.
more: v. n. To separate; to be separated (as plaster from a wall); to slip, slide, rebound (as a sword striking a hard body obliquely); to be broken or interrupted (sleep).

def: s. m. ( उत् and r. चट Break, pierce, injure) The act of vexing or rendering sorrowful, vexation.
s. m. Joy, gladness.

v. n. S. उच्छवस् to breathe, H. o'chhā narrow, hence short breath. To be nearly choked in the act of swallowing; to go down the wrong way. 1. Ok banā-kar pīne se, uchchhū ho gayā . 2. Yād khāne meṅ jo, Raṅgīṅ, mujhe kal tū āyā. To bachī mar hī ke, aisā mujhe uchchhū āyā . Wom. Rangīn. As yesterday at dinner to my mind you came, Rangīn, I very nearly died, I had such a fit of choking.

n. f. 1. Leaping and jumping; skipping; gambols. Kyā uchhal kūd kartā hai! nichlā baiṭh . Wom. 2. Merriment; revelry; enjoyment; rejoicing ( nāch raṅg ). Roṭī se jiskā nāk talak peṭ hai bharā, Kartā phire hai kyā woh uchhal kūd jā ba-jā! Nazīr. Who's stuffed up to the throat with food, Goes bounding, dancing all about.

E. n. f. H. uchhāl + chhek pudeṇdum muliebre . A wanton who exposes her bosom; a lustful woman; a harlot. Woh kutyā baṛī uchhāl-chhakkā hai, hatelī pe lȳë phirtī hai . That b—is always in heat, she carries it about in her palm.
v. n. See اچهلنا uchhalnā.
n. m. A festival; jubilee; public demonstration of joy; a grand procession of an image.
v.n. To withdraw (from, -se), to tire or be weary (of); to be offended (with), &c. ( = ućaṭnā, q.v.).
n. f. Opp. of bhīṛ , H. chhaṛā alone. Paucity; smallness; a small number.
v.t. To cause to be thrown up, &c.; also = ućhālnā, q.v. v.t. To throw up, toss up; to dandle, fling up (a child); to cast up, vomit; to toss about, bandy, treat with disrespect, bring discredit on
more: (caus. of اچهالنا) v. a. To order another to throw up any thing.
subs: v.t. (intens. of ućhālnā), To throw or toss up, &c.

def: v. a. 1. To separate; divide; part. 2. To cause to rebound, or recoil. 3. To cause to splash, spatter, scatter. 4. To turn one's mind from; divert; take off; turn aside, dissuade. Lagā lagāyā gāhak uchṭā dȳā . Just as the purchaser was on the point of buying, (that fellow) dissuaded him. 5. To depress; damp; discourage; dishearten ( dil toṛnā ). nīṅd uchṭānā, yā uchkānā , v. a. To disturb, or break one's sleep. Math thī sobat, āë jagāë daī, re, Ādhī rāt saīyāṅ sajyā merī āë, Kar chūmā-chāṭī, nīṅd uchkāë daī, re . Rus. Song. I was asleep, he came and woke me, dear, At midnight to my bed my husband came, He pressed my lips with kisses, broke my sleep.
adj. Proper, meet, just, right, fit, becoming, seemly, befitting, suitable; convenient; expedient, obligatory, necessary; acceptable; advisable.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit उचित (ucitá).

def: s.f. Propriety, fitness, befittingness, obligatoriness, &c. (See ućit.)
s.m. The reel on which the silk with which kites are flown is wound.
more: W. n. f. A wheel to roll the string on, in kiteflying.

n. One who suddenly snatches up, or forcibly makes off with a thing; a shop-lifter; a sharper. Uchakke ne nath khasoṭ lī . Rus. uchakkā-pan',—pan'ā , n. m. Taking one in; sharp practice; juggle; trickery; fraud.
s. m. A thief, a pickpocket.

def: s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, q.v. s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
v.t. To lift or raise up; to jerk up; to shrug (the shoulders).
more: Tir. v. a. S. उच्च Pr. uchchak high. To lift; raise up ( uṭhānā ); to shrug (one's shoulders). 1. Zarā aur uchkā do . Raise it up a little higher. 2. Kyoṅ khave uchkātā hai? Why do you shrug your shoulders?
v. a. To lift or raise up.

def: v.t. To cause to be separated (one thing from another), to have (one thing) separated (from another).
more: v. a. To order a person to separate (one thing from another).

def: s.m. A moth ( = patang).

v.t. = اچاٿنا ućāṭnā, q.v. v.t. To drive or turn away (from), to scare away; to dissuade (from); to banish, to break or disturb (sleep); to separate, divide; to cause to rebound; to cause to splash; to dishearten, discourage; to sadden, to make anxious — ućṭā-denā = ućāṭnā intensified. (See ućṭānā.)

s.m. An otter; a disputed point (this sense originates from a story of otters disputing over a distribution of their prey); — a stupid fellow, dolt, blockhead, fool, simpleton (cf. ūt); — adj. Voracious (as an otter), ready to devour; ready, eager
more: n. m. S. उद An otter. ūd bilāo kī ḍherī , n. m. 1. A disputed point that cannot be settled. ☞ The story is that several otters, catching fish in company, pile up their prey, in the first instance, in one heap, and afterwards make an equal division. Any one of the number however, who fancies his own heap to be smaller, will throw the whole again into one heap and divide again, and this goes on repeatedly. 2. ( ūt bilāo ) A stupid person; a blockhead; fool; simpleton; a goose.
adj. Ready; eager. Khāne ko ūd, kamāne ko majnūṅ . Prov. Ready to eat, lazy to work.
n. m. A wooden roller for breaking clods in the field.
s. m. An otter. See اودبلاو.

def: s.m. Wood, timber; a sprig; a staff, stick, rod; — aloes-wood; — a lute
more: n. m. A return; relapse. aud k. , v. a. 1. To get a relapse ( ubharnā , 6). 2. To return to; fall back upon.
n. m. The wood aloes. ūd-soz , n. m. A vessel in which ūd is burned.

s. m. 1. Wood, timber. 2. A staff, a stick. 3. The wood aloes. (In dakh. this word, however, seems used for gum benzoin). 4. A lute or harp. ¶ aﻌud, s. m. 1. Returning. 2. A man of abilities, experience, and prudence. عود سوز ūﻌd-soz, s. An utensil to burn wood of aloes (or gum benzoin) in.
s. (In dakh.) Styrax benzoin ( Qanoone-islam ): benjamin ( Harris ).
subs: s.m. A vessel in which aloes-wood is burned (for fumigation).

def: insep. prefix = उत् ut, q.v.
adj. Grey; brown; purple; — s.m. Grey, brown, or purple colour (see audāt).
more: s. m. Brown. adj. Of a brown colour.
adj. (dakh.) Light blue: see عوده ūﻌda.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀸𑀬𑀇 (uddāyaï, “is polished, is very clear”), from an unattested Sanskrit *उद्दात (*uddāta, “cleansed, bright, clear”), from उद्- (ud-, “over, above”) + दात (dāta, “cleansed, purified”), the latter from the root दै (dai, “to purify, cleanse”).

def: s.m. The ocean.

n. m. S. उत् up, इ to go. 1. Ascent; dawn; sunrise. 2. The East, in opposition to asta (West). 3. The eastern mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise. 4. Light; splendour; prosperity. udaë asta , adv. 1. From sunrise to sunset; the whole day. 2. From east to west; the whole world. Udaë asta tumhārā rāj ho . Benediction. udaë honā , v. n. 1. To emerge; appear; come in sight; to be visible; come into view; to be manifest; to reveal itself. Māyā more Rām kī, ude hot, chhup jāë . Song. Wealth is my Rām's, it comes and goes. 2. To begin; arise; take its rise; open; dawn; originate in, or from; commence; spring; emanate; set in; rise. Sūraj udaë huā ( yā niklā ). 3. To burst forth; crop out; peep out; start up; present itself; show its face. 4. To sprout; spring up; germinate; bud. 5. To result from; come into existence; to be derived from; to be caused by; to flow; accrue. 6. To come upon; fall upon. Mere to pāp udaë ho gaë, jo aise ke palle baṅdhī . Hin. Wom. My sins have borne fruit, since I am tied to such a one.
s.m. Example, illustration, instance; simile; comparison; an opposite argument; (in Logic) one of the five modes of reasoning.
more: n. m. A. مثال S. उत् over, आ all round, हृ to take. 1. An illustration, or example; a case in point; instance. 2. One of the five modes or rules of logical reasoning.
s. m. An example, a simile, a comparison.

def: s.f. = اداهت udāhat, q.v. s.f. Brownness; brown; purple.
s. f. (from اودا ūdā ) Brownness.
s.f. Brownness; brown; purple.
E. Wom. n. f. From ūdā . Purple color.
s.f. Brownness, &c. = udāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Brownness; brown; purple.
more: s. f. See ادا adā. ادائي دین adā-i dain, s. m. Payment of debt.

def: s. f. (from اودا ūdā ) Brownness.
s.m. Water; the ceremony of pouring water on obsequies; ablution.
s.m. One of the three or five vital airs (that which rises up the throat and passes into the head).
adj. Noble, gentle; illustrious; generous, liberal; munificent; great; best; — unperplexed
subs: adj. Of liberal dealing; of a generous disposition, noble-minded.
wety: From Sanskrit उदार (udāra).

def: s.m. Belly, stomach; bowels; womb; cavity; the interior or inside of anything; 'the inner man'; protuberance; 'corporation'; — slaughter, war
subs: (i.e. udara+āmay), s.m. Disease of the belly; dysentery; diarrhœa
adj. & s.m. Demon-bellied; voracious; gluttonous; — a glutton
s.f. The line of hair on the belly
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: corr. of ادهر udhar, q.v. adv. There, thither, on that side; that way; away
more: n. m. 1. Belly; stomach; bowels; womb ( peṭ ). Mās khāë, mās baṛhe; sāg khāë, oj baṛhe . Prov. Flesh makes flesh; potherbs make a large belly. 2. Food; livelihood; living ( bhojan ). Apne apne udar kī chintā ko gaë . Rājnītī. 3. Protuberance; bulge; enlargement. udar bhar'nā, peṭ bhar'nā , v. a. To fill one's stomach; have a belly full. Dukh dhandā kar-ke uddar bhar letī hūṅ . Hin. Wom. With the sweat of my brow, I fill my belly. udar pīṛā, peṭ kā khalal , n. Pain in the stomach; spasms; colic.
subs: adv. From that, or the other, side; that way
v.n. To turn away the ear; turn a deaf ear.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: = H ادڙاگدڙا उदड़ा गुदड़ा udṛā-gudṛā, گدڙ or گدڙا redupl.), s.m. rags and tatters; old rags; cast-off clothes.
adj. Abdominal; pregnant; &c. = udarin, q.v.: — s.f. Enlargement of the abdomen from dropsy or from flatulency.
adj. Having a large belly; pot-bellied, fat, corpulent; abdominal. (See udarī.)
adj. Having a large belly; pot-bellied, fat, corpulent; abdominal. (See udarī.)
s.f. A corpulent woman; a pregnant woman.
s.f. Liberality, generosity, munificence.

adj. Indifferent (to, -se), unconcerned, apathetic; unsettled in mind; retired, lone, solitary; forlorn, dejected, sad, sorrowful; dull, dispirited, cast-down; grieved, displeased (with); sullen, cross; not bright, dull, sombre, faded
more: adj. S. उत् + आस् to sit separate or away. 1. In low spirits; dispirited; cast-down; disheartened; dejected; doleful; pensive; sad; despondent; cheerless; down-cast; depressed; dull; low-spirited; out of spirits, or sorts. Kuchh ummed dil meṅ, kuchh-ek jī ko yās, Laboṅ par haṅsī, lek chehra udās . Mīr Hasan. Some hope she had, some feeling of despair; Her lips all smiles, but sadness on her face. 2. Sullen; sulky; cross; dumpish; mumpish. Phr. Down in the mouth; sick at heart; in the sulks; in the dumps; melancholy as a gib cat; a prey to melancholy. 3. Not bright; sombre; faded; dull ( māṅd ). Dupaṭṭe kā raṅg udās hai. udās ho'nā,—baiṭh'nā—paṛ'nā , v. n. 1. To be sad, dejected, downcast. Phr. To take to heart; to hang down the head; to prey on the mind, or spirits. 1. Mero balmā kāro, aur sab ke haiṅ gore, Maiṅ hot hūṅ udās dekh kāre ko . Rus. Song. My husband's dark, while all such fai…
s. Retiredness. adj. Retired, lonely, forlorn, dejected, sorrowful, sad.
subs: retiredness, loneliness; asceticism; — sickly crop (in agricult.)
udās paṛnā or paṛ-jānā, and udās honā, v.n. To be sad, sorrowful, dejected, &c.; to be grieved (at, -se), displeased (with); to turn (against), revolt (from); to be dull, dim, or lustreless.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi اداس / उदास (udāsa, “indifferent”) (c. 1503), a learned borrowing from Sanskrit उदास (udāsa).

def: adj. (dakh.) See اداس udās.
s.m. A cord worn as a shoulder-belt by Mohammadan devotees (āzād).
more: n. m. A cord worn as a shoulder belt by āzāds. udāsā kas'nā , v. n. To start; depart; leave; take one's way.

def: s.f. Sadness, sorrow; despondency, dejection; retirement; loneliness; solitude; dulness, dimness
more: n. f. Sadness; despondency; dullness; dejection. udāsī chhā'nā,—baras'nā, udāsā chhā nā , v. n. 1. To be clouded with sorrow. 2. To become dull, or cheerless. 1. Rājā jī! rānī tumheṅ mahloṅ rahī bulāë. Kal se tabiat sust hai, rahī udāsī chhāë . Wom. Play. Rūp Basaṅt . O king, the queen doth in the palace for you call, Since yesterday she's low, and gloom hangs over all. udāsī , n. m. 1. A hermit; anchorite; a class of faqīrs; one who has retired from the world, whose family is unknown, and who has neither friend nor foe. Pīu ke bairāg kī udāsī sūṅ, Dil yu bairāgī o udāsī haī . Walī. With sorrow from her lover torn, A widowed heart. downcast, forlorn. 2. One who eschews the married state for the sake of religion. Tum udāsī họ, ki gharbārī?
1. s. f. Solitude, loneliness, sadness, dejection. 2. ( उदासी ) adj. Lonely, retired, dejected. 3. ( उदासीन ) s. m. An hermit, anchorite; a class of faḳīrs.
subs: v.n. Dejection or gloom to cloud (the face); to be clouded with sorrow; to become dull or sad.
wety: From اُدَاس (udās) + ـِی (-ī).

def: adj. Unsettled; retired, solitary, lonely; dejected, forlorn, sad; — s.m. A dejected person, &c.; ascetic; anchorite; hermit; a class of religious mendicants; (in Music) the bass
wety: From اُدَاس (udās) + ـِی (-ī).

def: adj. Sitting apart; indifferent; free from affection: s.m. One who is completely detached from the world, whose family is unknown, and who has neither friend nor foe; hermit; misanthrope; stoical indifferent person; one who is neither a friend nor a foe; an umpire; an order of religious mendicants in the Panjāb.
s.f. Indifference, carelessness, apathy.

s.m. Rising (of the sun, &c.); dawn, sunrise; ascent; appearance, advent; the East (as opposed to asta, the West); the eastern mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise ( = udayāćal); light, splendour; prosperity, success
subs: (i.e. udaya+aćala), s.m. The mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise
( = S. udayāsta), udayast-tak, adv. From sunrise to sunset, the whole day; from the East to the West, over the whole world
To rise (as the sun, &c.), to dawn; to appear, be manifest; to begin, arise, originate; to spring up, sprout.

def: = उदय uday, q.v. s.m. Rising (of the sun, &c.); dawn, sunrise; ascent; appearance, advent; the East (as opposed to asta, the West); the eastern mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise ( = udayāćal); light, splendour; prosperity, success
subs: (i.e. udaya+aćala), s.m. The mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise
( = S. udayāsta), udayast-tak, adv. From sunrise to sunset, the whole day; from the East to the West, over the whole world
To rise (as the sun, &c.), to dawn; to appear, be manifest; to begin, arise, originate; to spring up, sprout.

def: adj. Rising, ascending; prosperous, flourishing.

= ادويگ udveg, q.v. s.m. Perturbation, anxiety, trouble, care, solicitude, perplexity, agitation, uneasiness; bewilderment; fear, apprehension, consternation; exertion; haste, hurry, impetuosity; — the fruit of the Areca catechu, betel nut.
more: n. S. उत beyond, वेग quick. 1. Perturbation; agitation; anxiety; uneasiness; restlessness; perplexity; care. 2. Fear; consternation; apprehension ( ḍar ). 3. Hurry; haste; impetuousness ( jaldī ). 4. The fruit of the areca catechu . Wat; betel-nut.

def: adj. 1. Perplexed; anxious; perturbed; agitated; at a loss; not knowing what to do. 2. Hasty; precipitate; impetuous.
s.m. A being born; springing up (as plants from seed); birth, production.
s. m. Act of being born or of springing up (as plants from seed), birth, production.
adj. Sprouting, germinating; vegetable.
s.m. See s.v. ūd.
more: s. m. An otter. See اود ūd.

def: s.m. Recollection; recognition.
s.m. = اديم udyam, q.v. s.m. Effort, exertion, endeavour; labour, toil, pains, trouble; employment, calling, business, trade
more: n. m. S. उद्यम् to strive, support. 1. Exertion; effort; endeavour ( koshish ). Aisan uddam karau ki mālik khush okhai . Bhoj. Exert yourself so that the master may be pleased. 2. Trouble; pains; toil; labour ( mehnat ). Uddam se daliddar ghate . Prov. Poverty declines before labour. 3. Calling; trade; business; avocation ( dhandā ). Taiṅ kaun uddam kare saë . Rus. uddam kar'nā, uddyam —, v. n. 1. To labour; work; toil; drudge; fag. Uddam karibā, tab na khaibā? Mag. Wom. Unless you work, how shall you eat? [The force of the peculiar construction of the latter clause is untranslatable]. 2. To strain; sweat; take pains or trouble. 3. To pull; tug; hammer at. 4. To drive a trade; enter a profession; spend time upon; make it one's business; perform the office or functions of ( dhandā k .).
subs: To use effort or exertion, to exert oneself, to endeavour, take pains or trouble; to labour, toil; to follow a trade or calling.

def: adj. Rebellious, in revolt
subs: To rebel, revolt, rise in rebellion, &c.

def: s.m. Direction, indication, sign, trace; search, inquiry; scope; ascertainment; report, information, tidings; exemplification, example; quarter, region, place, foreign land or country ( = pardes).
s.m. Anything to which one refers or which one has in view; anything noteworthy; (in Gram.) the subject.
s.m. A disease occasioned by the mounting of wind upwards; act of gasping or panting; asthma; flatulency; indigestion; plethora of the vessels of the head; headache.
more: s. m. Asthma, act of gasping or panting, plethora in the vessels of the head, headach, indigestion.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अधिक (adhika).

def: s.m. Debt; loan; trust, credit; obligation; deduction; discharge, release, extrication, deliverance, redemption, salvation; raising up from any place or state
more: n. m. S. उद + हृ to take up. Loan; debt or credit; accommodation; advance. 1. Nau naqad, na terah udhār . Prov. Nine cash better than thirteen due. 2. Khair kī jūtī, khairāt kā nāṛā, Paṛh de, Mullā, aqad udhārā . Prov. These trousers and the shoes she's on, in charity were got. On tick, do you, we pray, good Mullā , tie the nuptial knot. 3. Kyā udhār kī māṅ marī hai? Prov. Is the race of credit extinct? i. e. Credit can always be had somewhere or other. (Usually said by the disappointed applicant). udhār de'nā , v. a. 1. To lend, or give on credit; to advance money. 2. To put out at interest. Sadqā dȳā, rad balā, Udhār dȳā, gāhak gagā . Prov. The offering made, the evil spirit's flown, Give credit, and your customer is gone. [His cash purchases are made elsewhere]. — de'ne-wā'lā , n. A lender; creditor; obliger. udhār khā'nā , v. n. 1. To live on credit. 1. Āg khāë, muṅh jare; udhār kh…
s. m. Food, aliment, victuals, nourishment, support.
s. m. Debt (especially not bearing interest), deduction, discharge, deliverance, salvation, raising up from any place or state.
subs: v.t. To lend; give on credit, or give credit; to discharge a debt
v.t. To rescue, extricate (from, -se), save, &c.; to effect the removal (of, -kā); to remove, do away with
(-ke ūpar), To treat (one) as a mortal enemy, to become (one's) mortal enemy
v.t. To contract a debt; to borrow, take a loan; to take on credit
v.t. To ask on loan, to borrow
uddhār or udhār honā or ho-jānā
To be in debt; to owe; to be out on loan, be lent; to be released (from, -se, an obligation, &c.); to be liberated, discharged; to be delivered or extricated (from danger, difficulties, &c.).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀤𑁆𑀥𑀸𑀭 (uddhāra), from Sanskrit उद्धार (uddhārá, “act of raising”).

def: adj. & s.m. Delivering, liberating, relieving; — deliverer, liberator, &c.
v.t. To deliver, liberate, discharge, extricate (from, -se).
more: (see ادهار udhār ) v. a. To liberate, to discharge.

def: part. adj. Puffed up, vain, haughty, arrogant, rude, ill-behaved; — s.m. A king's wrestler.
s.m. Blazing or glowing of a luminous body; illumination; exciting, flaming (as passion).
part. Illuminated.
Wom. n. f. H. uṛnā, uḍnā to fly, S. उत् up, डी to fly. A woman who is always gadding about. Uḍḍo kā ek pal kām pe dīdā nahīṅ lagtā . Hin. Wom. Not one moment does this gad-about attend to her work.

v. n. Mah. Wom. To become disgraced, contemptible; to lose one's good name. 1. Apne kautakoṅ se uḍdū uḍḍū ho rahī hai . Wom. From her bad conduct she has become a byeword. 2. Milte hī devar se sab meṅ uḍḍū uḍḍū ho gaī maiṅ. Unke aib chhupeṅge, bī, jo log daulat rakhte haiṅ . Wom. Nāzaīn. Through sorting with my husband's younger brother I've become A scandal. Sister, hushed the faux pas are of those who've wealth.

ety: from uḍṅā; ū or o = S. affix उक
def: s.m.f. One who goes flying about from place to place, a gad-about
subs: To become disgraced; to lose one's good name.

def: s.m. Pointing to or at, direction; track; search, quest

E. n. m. S. उत् out; देश country, उद्देश information. 1. Foreign parts; abroad; another province or country ( par-des ). 2. News; intelligence; tidings; in formation ( saṅdesā ). 1. Wā kā udes nā jāne . E. Rus. 2. Kuchh bhāge ke udeswā ho, hamrā ke dehū nā . Mag. Song. O, if you aught know of the run-away, pray tell me, do! 3. Sign; trace; address; direction. Saīyāṅ ke udeswā batā de, batohī, kene jāūṅ? Mag. Song. Wayfarer, tell me of my husband, pray, Whither, ah whither shall I wend my way?
s. m. (dakh. perhaps for उद्देश ) History, explanation, state, condition (?).
s.m. Throwing out, spitting out; vomiting, belching; saliva, eructation; exhalation; sound; roar.
part. Thrown up, vomited; risen up, sprung up, grown.
s.m. Opening, unlocking; opener, instrument for opening (as a key, &c.); the rope and bucket of a well, a leather bucket used for drawing water.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Done with effort; exerted; opened, unlocked.

r: uḍhāl'nā, ḍhāl'nā, uṛhālnā, ḍhulkā'nā, uṛhīk'nā, bheṛ'nā
def: E. v. a. To shut (the door).
v.n. To be ruined, destroyed, spoiled; to be squandered, dissipated, &c.; to be carried off, to run away, elope, be seduced (a woman); to be full of lust, long for carnal intercourse (a woman)
subs: and is the more common form.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *उद्ध्वलति (*uddhvalati), from उद्- (ud-) + ह्वल॑ति (hválati, “to go astray”), or from उद्- (ud-) + *ध्वलति (*dhvalati).

def: v. n. See ubhar'na=ū'par ā'nā . 1. To be brimful of lust; to be in heat; to feel an uncontrollable desire for sexual intercourse. 1. Mat pūchh, dadā, tū koka ko "kyoṅ ubhrī ubhrī phirtī hai." Bhar jo gaī hai mastī meṅ, to udhlī udhlī phirtī hai , Wom. 2. Dhī, na beṭī, udhal gaī samdheṭī . Prov. No child, no daughter, and she cries the daughter-in-law's gone wrong. 3. Udhlī jātī hai nigoṛī marduoṅ ko dekh-kar, Kab talak kokā kī tū, āchā, khabar-dārī kare? Wom. Rangīn. 2. To be seduced; to run away with a man. Beṭī udhal gaī, shekhī nikal gaī . Since the daughter has run away, the conceit has been taken out of them (the parents). 3. To be spoiled, marred, E. ( bigaṛnā ). Kām udhal gayā . E.
s.m. Noise, uproar ( = dhūm), disturbance, commotion, ado, tumult, rebellion; quarrel, brawl
more: s. m. Noise, impudence, rebellion, disturbance.
subs: ūdham karnā, ūdham maćānā, v.n. To make a great noise, raise a commotion, make an ado, &c.

def: n. m. S. उत् + धू to shake up. 1. Noise; uproar; disturbance; tumult ( hāhū ). 2. Wrangle; brawl; quarrel; fight. ūdham jot'nā, machānā, uṭhānā, yā karnā v. a. To make a great noise, row, etc. 1. Jab dardeṅ lagī thīṅ to kyā kuchh kam raṅg lāīṅ thīṅ, thoṛā ūdham jotā thā? E. Wom. When you were in the throes of child birth no little fuss you made. Didn't you scream and yell! 2. Manhaī hāmār chuppī lagāë rahā hai kālh, Udham uṭhāī rāt tihāre bhatār ne . E. Wom. A double entendre . Jān Sāhab. What a row your husband made last night, my man kept very quiet.
adj. Noisy, riotous, turbulent; quarrelsome; — s.m. A noisy person, &c.
more: adj. Noisy; turbulent; quarrelsome; riotous. Udhmī bachche ne sārā ghar sir par uṭhā liyā . Wom.

def: s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, q.v. s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
adv. There, thither, on that side; that way; away
more: adv. There, thither, on that side. ادهرسي udhar se, From that, or the other, side.
subs: adv. From that, or the other, side; that way
v.n. To turn away the ear; turn a deaf ear.

def: adv. There, thither, on that side, on the other side: see ادهر u-dhar.
s.m. Debtor.

v.n. To be taken out or off; to be or become unrolled, unravelled, untwisted, unwound; to be torn off, be laid or ripped open, be excoriated, peeled, flayed (the skin); to open out, unravel, come undone (sewing); to be untied, be separated, be divorced; to be ruined or destroyed; to be spent, exhausted, dissipated, squandered
subs: and is the more common form.

def: v. n. S. दृ to tear asunder. 1. To be unrolled, untwisted, unravelled, or unwound. Lāo, yā pechak, udhaṛ gaī . 2. To be opened out (the stitches, or seam of a garment). Sārī silāī udhaṛ gaī . 3. To be flayed, peeled off (the skin). Khāl udhaṛ gaī . 4. To be beaten, flogged, caned, whipped, etc. Khelo, chachā, chamṛī udhṛegī, jab mālūm hogī . Play away, my boy—wont you catch it? you'll know then. (Said by one schoolboy to another). 5. To be untied, separated, divorced. Kal nikāh buṅdhā, āj udhaṛ gayā . 6. To be impoverished, ruined, undone. Ṭikas ke māre khalqat udhaṛ gaī . The whole country has been ruined by this (income) tax.

s.m.f. (?) Unravelling; unweaving; unpicking (stitches), &c.: udheṛ-bun, s.f. Lit. 'Unpicking and weaving'; (hence), perplexity, bewilderment, embarrassment, confusion, dilemma; uneasiness, disquiet, anxiety, solicitude, concern; distress, grief; — devising, planning; deliberation, anxious thought; scheme, plan, device.
more: s. Unravelling, unweaving. ادهیڙ بن udheṛ-bun, Perplexity.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀅𑀤𑁆𑀥𑀯𑁂𑀟 (*addhaveḍa), from Sanskrit अर्ध (ardha, “half”) + *वेड (*veḍa) with the second term possibly related to वेला (velā, “time, occasion”). Cognate with Punjabi ਅਧੇੜ (adheṛ), Gujarati આધેડ (ādheḍ).

def: n. f. H. udheṛ pick to pieces, bun weave. 1. Deliberation; working of the mind; calculation; anxious thought. Mujhe rāt din is kī shādī kī udheṛ bun rahtī hai . 2. Scheme; machination; plot; plan. 3. Restlessness; disquiet; uneasiness; anxiety; care; trouble; concern ( sāṅsā ). Apnī udheṛ-bun na gaī bād-i-marg bhī, Ham qabr meṅ bhī tār-i-kafan sāṅṭte rahe . ☞ The notion of an ardent lover, in his restlessness, alternately unravelling ( udheṛnā ) and weaving ( bunnā ) the threads of his shroud, is taken from the Mahomedan belief that two angels ( nakīrain ) cause the dead man to sit up in his grave and examine him as to his faith and his ability to distinguish between a true and a false likeness of Mahomed, both of which are put before him. 4. Confusion; perplexity; dilemma; embarrassment. Kyā hai, kis udheṛ-bun meṅ ghabrāë phirte ho? 5. Distress; grief.
v. a. See ادهيڙنا udheṛnā.

v.n. = udhaṛnā, q.v.; — 2˚ v.t. To undo, unsew, unravel, unpick (sewing), untwist, unknot; to pull to pieces; to lay open, unfold, disclose, expose; to strip off, skin, flay, pluck, rip, excoriate, bark; to pull off the upper part of (as the crust of a cake, or the roof of a house); to pull up; open again (as a grave, &c.); to ruin, destroy; to impoverish, reduce to poverty; to waste, squander, dissipate, scatter, make away with, plunder
more: v. a. To undo, unravel, unfold.
v. a. To strip off (a skin), rip, skin, excoriate: see the same word in the foregoing part.
subs: intens. of udheṛnā, v.t. To pluck out, pull up or off, rip open, lay quite bare, &c. (see udheṛnā).

def: = ادهيڙنا udheṛnā, q.v.
more: v. a. S. उद् out, हृ to take. 1. To undo; unsew; open out; unpick; pull, or pick to pieces; unlace. 1. Sīwan udheṛo . 2. Khāṭ udheṛ-ke bun lo . 3. Sauṛ udheṛ-ke kāt le . Hin. Wom. 2. To untwist; unravel; untwine; unknot (suljhānā). Rassī ke bal udheṛ ḍāl . 3. To ravel; knot; tangle ( uljhānā ). E. Hai dāī! appan nenā samhārū, hammar janaū udheṛ delak . Tir. Nurse, take away your baby, here's my janeu all in knots. 4. To pluck; skin; strip; rip open; flay; take off the upper layer of a thatch, roof, etc.; to dismantle. 1. Bakrī ko udheṛ lo . Mah. 2. Roṭī udheṛ-kar na khāo, naṅgī hotī hai . Wom. ☞ The women have a superstition that to take off the upper layer of the chapātī is to dishonor it by exposing the inner portion, and hence to bring misfortune on the offending person. 3. Sārī chhat udheṛ ḍālī . 5. To pluck out (all the feathers in fight). Hence, to defeat; conquer. Murge ne mārte m…

def: v. a. To undo, unravel, unfold.
adj. (f. of udhlā), Lascivious, lustful, lecherous.
more: adj. Lecherous; lascivious; lustful; lickerish. Udlī bahū baleṅḍe sāṅp dikhāë . Prov. The gadding wife sees a snake in the roof. (An excuse for running out of the house).

def: s. m. Name of a friend and companion of Kr̤ish̤ṇa.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Unrolled, unravelled, undone, untied; excoriated, peeled, flayed, &c. (See next.)
= ادهڙا udhṛā, q.v.
more: adj. Opened, undone, unrolled (as the seam in a garment, or thread which was rolled up in a clue).

def: s.f. A light shade of blue or grey, or of purple.
adj. Of wood; of aloes-wood.
more: adj. Made of the wood aloes.
adj. Of wood or of the wood aloes.

def: part. adj. Risen (as the sun, &c.); shining; distinguished, conspicuous (as valour, &c.).
more: adj. Risen (as the sun), distinguished, conspicuous (as valour, &c.).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Elevated, high; acutely accented; great, illustrious; generous, liberal, bountiful; dear, beloved; — s.m. The acute accent; a high or sharp tone; an ornament in rhetoric; a kind of musical instrument; a large drum; a gift or donation.
W. Hin. n. m. A vessel carved out of stone.
n. f. A small vessel carved out of stone.
s.m. Madness, mania; extravagance; frenzy; excitement; lust, lasciviousness; pride, conceit; show, ostentation; vice, depravity
more: Tir. n. m. S. उन्माद, उत excessive, मद् to be drunk. 1. Lust; heat; lechery; lasciviousness; wantonness; concupiscence. 2. Restlessness; agitation; anxiety. 3. Wildness; madness; mania; frenzy. 4. Pride; conceit; arrogance. Sab mad madaī hai, bidyā mad udmād . Prov. Spirits intoxicate, but learning most of all. 5. Vice; evil; depravity. Nīch zāt, ek na ek udmād . Prov. The base born have some vice or other. 6. Ostentation; affectation; display. udmād lag'nā , v. n. Wom. 1. To lust after; long for; to be prurient, rakish, etc. 2. To hanker after things beyond one's means. 3. To affect; to show off; mince.
subs: v.n. To be stirred with morbid desire, lust or lasciviousness; to lust (after); hanker (after); to be filled with affectation; to make a display; to swagger.

def: adj. & s.m. Mad, frantic; morbidly desirous, lustful, lascivious, libidinous, lewd, wanton
adj. & s.m. Mad, frantic; morbidly desirous, lustful, lascivious, libidinous, lewd, wanton
more: Māṛ. adj. Prurient; lustful; lickerish; lascivious; libidinous; lewd; wanton. Bāt mujh ko nahīṅ ye khush ātī, Aisī bandī nahīṅ hai udmātī . Wom. Shauq. udmā'dī, udmā'tī, udmā'dā , Wom. n. A sensualist; debauchee; rake; libertine; a man of pleasure.

def: v.n. = اڙنا uṛnā, q.v. v.n. To fly, fly up, soar; to take flight; to move or go rapidly or quickly, to go flying along; to disappear, vanish; to be sprung; to explode (a mine); to spread, become current, be circulated (a report or rumour); to make a show or display, cut a dash; give oneself airs; to be in full bloom, to flourish; to try one's pranks or tricks (with, -se), to practise deceit or fraud (with); to be dexterously or skilfully handled, &c. (an instrument or tune)
more: adj. compar. (of دني) 1. Inferiour, lowest, mean, small, little, trivial, unimportant; a person of no consequence. 2. Nearest.
subs: v.n. ( = uṛnā, but generally intensive, e.g.), to fly away; to run away, escape; to be wiped out, rubbed out, effaced, obliterated; to be worn out; to be lost; to be exhausted, spent; to disappear, vanish, to be carried off, stolen; to be dissipated; to escape, to evaporate (as scent, &c.); to become faded or dim; to be ravished; lose one's senses; to fly or rush (at, -par)
v.n. To walk with a stately or affected gait, to strut ( = itrānā); to grow vain or conceited (because of, or in, -meṅ)
To be in a state of bloom, to flourish.

def: s.f. Shine, light, lustre, radiance, splendour; rising (of the moon, &c.).
= ادت udit, q.v. part. adj. Risen (as the sun, &c.); shining; distinguished, conspicuous (as valour, &c.).
more: s. f. Shining, splendour, rising (of the moon, &c.).

def: s.m. Otter; crab; any aquatic animal.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: Māṛ. n. m. Cast off clothes; old rags ( chīthṛe bodṛe ). 1. Apne udṛe gudṛe uṭhā yāṅ se . Wom. 2. Udaṛ gudaṛ sobaī, marjād-vāle robaī . E. Prov. The ragged sleep, the down-comes weep.
= H ادڙگدڙ उदड़ गुदड़ udaṛ-gudaṛ, گدڙ or گدڙا redupl.), s.m. rags and tatters; old rags; cast-off clothes.
s.m. A wooden mortar used for pounding rice and separating the husk ( = okhlī, q.v.); — a gum resin, bdellium.

ety: inf. n. of عدل 'to decline, deviate, turn aside or away'
def: s.m. Declining, swerving, receding, returning; refusing; deserting; disobedience (of orders), insubordination
more: n. m. Disobedience of orders; evasion of process; insubordination. Lauṅḍi hūṅ, nahīṅ udūl mujh ko Khidmat meṅ karo qabūl mujh ko! Your slave I am, your orders I'll obey, My service but accept! with you I'll stay. udūl-hukm , n. f. One who is disobedient or refractory. udūl-hukmī k. , v. a. To disobey; to refuse to obey; to be refractory.
(from عدل) s. m. Declining, refusing, receding, deserting, returning. عدول حکمي کرنا ūﻌdul-ḥukm-ī-karnā, To disobey orders. عدول کرنا ūﻌdūl karnā, To deviate from, disobey, decline, refuse.
subs: s.m. One who is disobedient or refractory
s.f. Disobedience of orders, refractoriness, insubordination; evasion of a sentence or process
v.n. To disobey orders, to refuse to obey, to be refractory
(-se), To deviate (from), turn aside or away (from), to rebel (against), to disobey; to decline, refuse.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻādil, q.v.), Just men, &c.
s.m. The glomerous fig-tree (Ficus glomerata) or gūlar tree.
more: s. m. 1. The glomerous fig-tree (Ficus glomerata). 2. A sect of Brāhmans.

def: s.m. Raft, float; the moon.
s.m. Marriage, wedding.

s.m. Perturbation, anxiety, trouble, care, solicitude, perplexity, agitation, uneasiness; bewilderment; fear, apprehension, consternation; exertion; haste, hurry, impetuosity; — the fruit of the Areca catechu, betel nut.
more: s. m. Agitation, fear, consternation, anxiety, uneasiness, exertion.

def: adj. Perturbed, troubled, perplexed, distressed, anxious, agitated; bewildered; hasty, precipitate.
adj. Sorrowful, anxious, perplexed, troubled; dejected.
s.m. Effort, exertion, endeavour; labour, toil, pains, trouble; employment, calling, business, trade
subs: To use effort or exertion, to exert oneself, to endeavour, take pains or trouble; to labour, toil; to follow a trade or calling.

def: adj. Active, energetic, laborious, persevering; prompt; engaged in trade, &c.
s.m. Royal pleasure-ground, park or garden; — motive, purpose.
s.m. Bringing to a conclusion; closing ceremony; dedication of a temple, &c.
more: n. m. Closing ceremonies; the observance that winds up and completes any religious rite; the dedication of a temple, tank, etc. to a religious or benevolent purpose.

def: part. adj. Raised, held up; active, persevering, assiduous, diligent, laborious; eager, prepared, ready; — s.m. Section, chapter, division (of a book)
s.m. Effort, exertion, continued endeavour, perseverance; active preparation; plan, scheme
more: n. m. S. उद् out, युज् to join, apply. 1. Exertion; continued effort; perseverance. 2. Scheme; plan; contrivance ( upāë ). udyog' kar'nā , v. a. 1. To exert oneself; contrive, etc. Udyog kare, man-kāmnā pūran hoë . Rājnītī. Exert yourself, and your heart's desire shall be fulfilled. 2. To search after; look about.
subs: To exert oneself, strenuously endeavour; to seek diligently after; to contrive.

def: adj. Active, energetic, laborious, persevering.
s.m. Light, lustre.

intj. (expressive of anxiety, distress of mind, vexation, disgust, &c.), Oh! oh dear! alas! ugh! foh! faugh! fye! pish! pooh! — s.m. Dirt, filth, rubbish; a mere puff or blast of breath, &c. (in this sense the word does not occur alone, but in combination with the verbs kar-denā and ho-jānā; see below)
more: intj. 1. Oh! alas! oh dear! 2. Pshaw! tut! pooh! 1. Bal-be istignā! ki woh yāṅ āte āte rah gaë, Uf re be-tālī! ki yāṅ to dam hī niklā jāë hai . Zauq. Bravo, independence! so free to come or stay away; While here, alas, weak restlessness! my life doth ebb away. 2. Uf re be-dardī . 3. Uf re garmī. uf kar'nā , v. n. 1. To sigh; lament; to heave a deep sigh. 2. To pooh-pooh; scout at; not to care a pin or straw for; to hold cheap. 3. To burn to ashes; destroy; bring to nought. Sārā ghar jalā-ke uf kar dȳā uf na kar'nā, yā nikāl'nā , v. n. 1. Not to say a single word; to hold one's tongue; to endure in silence. 1. Us ne saikṛoṅ bāteṅ kahīṅ, maiṅ ne uf na kī . 2. Āre se agar chīre, to maiṅ uf nahīṅ kartā . Sheftā. Saw me in two, and no sound shall escape my lips. 2. To be indifferent to one's pain; to be hard-hearted, merciless, etc.; not to shew compassion. 1. Mar jāë, par uf na kare . Prov.…
interj. Fy! pish! for shame! alas! اف کرنا uf karnā, To disapprove of, to lament.
subs: v.n. To utter the exclamation uff by reason of disquietude, distress, &c.; to heave a sigh, to sigh, lament; to signify disapproval or contempt; to pooh-pooh (at), think lightly (of)
v.t. To reduce to dirt or dust, to ruin, destroy, reduce to nothing

uff na nikālnā, v.n. To make no sign indicative of distress, vexation, &c., not to breathe a syllable, to endure in silence; to make no sign of sympathy or compassion, to shew perfect indifference to distress, &c.
v.n. To be reduced to dirt or dust, to become ruined, destroyed, &c.

def: s.m. Horizon; a tract or region of the earth; (poet.) the world.
more: s. m. 1. The horizon. 2. A tract or region of the earth. 3. ( poet. ) The world.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افق (ufuq), from Arabic أُفُق (ʔufuq).

def: part. = افتاده uftāda, q.v.; — (as a v.n.) s.f. State of helplessness; — beginning of life, childhood.

ety: perf. part. of uftādan; Z. aibi+rt. pat; S. abhi+pat
def: part. adj. Fallen, lying flat or horizontally; lying waste or untilled (land); poor, wretched, helpless
more: adj. اُفتادن to fall. 1. Waste or untilled (land). uftā'dah , n. f. Lands culturable but not cultivated.

def: Now falling now rising; with much ado.
adv. Falling and getting up with great difficulty, &c. ( = girte-paṛte, q.v.).
adv. Ped. for H. girte paṛte , q. v.
s.f. Fall, act of falling; helplessness, weakness.

ety: for A. عفونه, inf. n. of عفن 'to become putrid,' &c.
def: s.f. Putridity, rottenness, corruption; stinking, stink, fetidness; effluvia; infection.
more: n. f. عفن rotting. Strong, foul, or unwholesome smell; effluvia ( badhū ). marz kī ufūnat . G. G. Infection of disease.
(from عفن) s. f. Corruption, infection, stink, fetidness.

ety: for A. عفوصة, fr. ʻafṣ, q.v.
def: s.f. Astringency; constipation; bitterness.
s.f. Collections, proceeds, produce, collection of rent or revenue; sum of money realized or paid by instalments; payment by instalments; dues, money to be realized; usury.
more: (from اگاهنا q. v.) s. f. 1. Usury. 2. The trade of lending money on interest of one fourth, the payment of which is received by instalments.

def: v.t. To gather, collect, accumulate; to realize, raise (money or funds), net; to tax, levy, impose; to beg (for), to importune (also written ughānā).
more: v. a. To gather, to collect, to accumulate.

def: v. a. See اُگهانا
s.m. That which is spit out after chewing anything (especially betel-leaf)
more: n. m. S. उद्गार spitting, Pr. uggār . What is spat out after chewing anything (especially betel leaf). ugāl-dān , n. m. A spittoon; a vessel for spitting in ( pīkdān ).

s. m. That which is spit out after chewing any thing (especially, betel leaf). آپ کا اگال میرا ادهار āp kā ugāl merā adhār, Your worshipʼs leavings are my nourishment; i e. You, Sir, are my support and benefactor.
subs: s.m. (or ugāl-dānī, s.f.), A vessel for spitting in, a spittoon (syn. pīkdān).

def: s.m. A certain insect which attacks standing crops; land always saturated with moisture; ooze, slime.
more: E. n. 1. An insect which attacks standing crops. 2. Land always saturated with moisture.

def: s. m. A spitting-box.
n. f. from اُگنا to rise. The first rays of the rising sun or moon.
v.t. To spit out; — v.n. To chew the cud, to ruminate ( = jugālnā).

v.t. To spit out; to bring up from the stomach or craw (as birds for their young, or as cattle in chewing the cud); to throw up, vomit, reject, disgorge; to restore or refund (property surreptitiously obtained = ugālnā, q.v.)
more: v. a. Lat. jugulum , H. galā the throat. S. गृ to swallow, उत् + गृ to eject. 1. To spit out; bring up from the stomach or craw (as birds for their young). 2. To cast up; throw off; disgorge; discharge; reject. 3. To give back; restore; return; refund (property surreptitiously obtained). zahar ugal'nā , v. n. lit . To vomit or eject poison. To shew or betray envy or jealousy.
v. a. To spit out, to vomit; (metaph.) to refund property surreptitiously obtained.

more: v. n. To be uncovered, discovered, opened, pulled off.

v. a. S. उत् + घट् to open. 1. To uncover; unveil; expose; open; bare; throw off one's covering or dress. Apnī ṭāṅg ughāṛye, aur āp hī lājoṅ marye . Prov. Expose your leg and die of shame. (To fom one's own nest). 2. To disclose; reveal; betray (one's secret). Ḍhakī dabī bāt ughāṛ dī . Wom.

Ped. n. m. The East. Ugman kānī unman ubhrā, pāṅṛī mūlan barsegā .
v.n. To grow, springup, shoot, sprout, germinate; to be produced, to rise, bud; to begin, set in; to dawn
more: v. n. S. उत् up, गम् to go. 1. To sprout; germinate; grow. Jab se uge bāl, tab se yehī hawāl . Prov. He has been in this state ever since his hair began to grow. 2. To spring; rise; bud; begin; dawn; set in. 1. Wohīṅ chāṅd peṛoṅ ke pīchhe ugā. Uṭhā lāl sā jagmagātā huā . Phūloṅ kā hār. 2. Ugtā hai mere dil meṅ tere pyār kā paudā . Nazīr. ugte hī jal jā'nā , v. n. To be withered or blasted at its birth; to be nipped in the bud

v. n. To grow, to be produced, to spring (as plants), to rise (as the moon). اگتي هي جل جانا ugte hī jal jānā, To be withered immediately on springing up (is applied to a hope or expectation which is blasted in the bud).
subs: To be withered or blasted at its birth, to be nipped in the bud.

def: pron. emphat. That very, &c. = uhi, wǒhī, q.v.
adj. Inferrible, to be supplied (in reasoning); elliptical; to be investigated.
intj. Expressing pleasure, pain, fear, surprise, &c. (used by women).
more: intj. Mah. Wom. O! Oh! fye! alas! dear me! ah me! 1. Ūhī, dar gor! kyā ye hāl huā! Wom. Jān Sāhab. 2. Ranḍi udhar ko, "uhī re"! kah-kar girī niḍhāl, Bhaṛwā udhar ko "āh re"! kah-kar phisal paṛā . The rains. Nazīr. The woman there screams out " ūhī " and falls upon her back, Her pimp gallant here cries out " āh ," slips down upon her track. 3. Āhe mardaṅ, na ūhī zanāṅ . Prov. Not a man's āhe , nor a woman's ūhī in him. (He is neither man nor woman—said to a great coward).

def: pron. (dakh.) That very: see وهي wuh-ī.

adj. Very apathetic, very cold, hard or unfeeling; very senseless, stupid or ignorant, intensely obtuse, idiotic; ill-mannered, unmannerly, uncouth, awkward, loutish, clownish, rude, rough, vulgar, coarse
subs: cold or unfeeling person; a very stupid fellow, an arrant blockhead, a fool; a clumsy, awkward fellow, a lout, an ill-mannered fellow, &c.
wety: Related to جڑ (jaṛ, “lifeless”).

def: adj. S. जड stupid. 1. Very stupid, ignorant, etc. Phr. Able to make nothing of. 1. Gaṅvār kyā, nirā ujaḍ hai . 2. Torā nīar ujaḍ kono naī khe . Mag. There is no such fool as you. 2. Ill-bred; ill-mannered; unmannerly; clownish. Tū kaisā ujaḍ hai, samhal-ke nahīṅ baiṭhtā . 3. Vulgar; coarse; rude; rough; awkward. Sab chhūṭe to chhūṭe, tor ujaḍ bolyā na chhūṭe . Mag. Whatever else you may leave off, you will never drop your rude speech.
more: n. m. 1. A fool; an arrant fool; a very stupid fellow. Are gaṅvār ke ujaḍ! tohe kahāṅ lo samjhāūṅ? 2. An unsympathetic, unfeeling person. Aìsan ujaḍwā se joṛalā sanehyā, daradyo na būjhe . Bhoj. Song. Jhūmar . To such a stony heart I have bound my love, he hasn't a particle of feeling.

def: adj. Light; bright, shining; splendid, gaudy; celebrated, famous; conspicuous, manifest; — s.m. Light, brightness, splendour.
more: adj. S. जागृ to wake, ujā'gar the sun. 1. Bright; shining; vivid; luminous; beaming; radiant; refulgent. 2. Splendid; celebrated; famous. 3. Conspicuous; manifest; clear; obvious. Yeh bāt jagat ujāgar ho gaī . jag' at ujā'gar , n. f. The light of the world; the sun.
s. m. Splendour, brightness. adj. Bright, splendid, famous.

def: s.m. Light, day-light, day-break, day; sunshine; splendour, brightness, refulgence; glory, pride, ornament; — adj. (f. -ī), Light, clear, bright, shining, luminous, &c.
more: n. m. S. ज्वल् to shine. 1. Day; day-break; sunshine; sunlight; the light of day; daylight; broad daylight; dawn; morning; break of day; glare. 2. Splendour; brightness; refulgence; flash; illumination; radiance; lustre; brilliancy. Binā dȳe naīṅ hot ujā'lā, sab se sār khairāt, Mȳāṅ . Mendicant's cry. 3. Glory; pride; ornament. 4. An only son. Aṅdhere ghar kā ujā'lā . The light of the house. ujālā ho'nā , v. n. 1. To be lighted; to become light. 2. To be empty or vacant (a house, by death or other calamity); to become dark, cheerless, still as death. ( Ujālā light, is here used for aṅdhërā darkness, which is esteemed a word of ill omen.) 3. To take fire; catch fire. Rare. (āg lagnā). ujā'le kā pah'rā , n. m. The last watch of the night; the watch which begins at dawn. ujā'le kā tā'rā , n. m. W. Sūkh . E. Bhurkā . The morning star; Venus.
1. s. m. Splendour, brightness, light. 2. adj. See اجل ujjal.
subs: To become light; to be lighted, (hence) to kindle up, take fire
s.m. The morning watch, the watch which begins at dawn
s.m. The morning star, the planet Venus.

adj. f. Bright: see اجل ujjal.
v.n. To be bright, be polished, to shine; to be or become clean.

def: v.t. To make bright; to light, light up, cause to shine; to brighten; to clean, polish, furbish; to make white
more: v. a. Caus. of ujalnā , q. v. 1. To light; light up. 2. To cause to shine or glisten; to brighten; make bright. 3. To clean; rub up; furbish; burnish; polish (metal or jewels). Gahnā ujāl do . 4. To clean; make white; wash. Rus. Kapṛā ujāl do . Rus.
(caus. of اجلنا q. v.) v. a. To cleanse, to polish (metals or jewels).
subs: v.t. To light up, rub up, brighten, polish; to cleanse, make white; to wash (prov.).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit उज्ज्वलयति (ujjvalayati). Compare Sindhi اُجرَڻُ (ujraṇu) / उजिरणु, اُجارَڻُ (ujāraṇu) / उजारणु, Vaghri ઊજારણુ (ūjārṇu), Gujarati ઉજાળવું (ujāḷvũ), અજવાળવું (ajavāḷvũ), Marathi उजळणे (ujaḷṇe), Odia ଉଜଳିବା (ujaḷibā), ଉଜାଳିବା (ujāḷibā).

def: adj. Laid waste, depopulated, demolished, ruined, in ruin, waste, desolate, &c. = ujāṛ, q.v.

adj. Desolate. See اُجار ūjaṛ karnā , v. a. See ujāṛ karnā . 1. Davāī davāī kahat haiṅ, aur kar matvāro det, Karwī aisī hot hai, aur ghar ūjaṛ kar det . Riddle. Ans Sharāb Wine. He calls it medicine, one's senses it o'ercomes, So bitter to the taste, it ruin brings on homes. 2. Chalīṅ janyān gaune, ūjaṛ ho gayo des, Kab tum, janyāṅ, jāne maike ko aī ho . E. Rus. Song. Gone to your husband's home, my life, this place a desert grown, When will my love return to her maternal home, her own? ūjaṛ kheṛā , n. m. A depopulated, deserted place. Ujaṛ kheṛā, nāo na beṛā . Prov A deserted village, nor boat nor raft.

def: adj. Laid waste, devastated, demolished, ruined, spoiled; depopulated, deserted, abandoned; waste, desolate (also written اوجڙ ūjaṛ)

def: (see اجڙنا) v. n. To become desolate.
v. a. (dakh.) To lay waste: see اجاڙنا ujāṛnā.

v. n. S. जृ to decay. 1. To be plucked up by the roots; to be destroyed root and branch. Sārā khet ujaṛ gayā . 2. To be destroyed, pulled down, razed to the ground, demolished; to come down ( girnā ), Aisī barsāt huī, bīsyoṅ ghar ujaṛ gaë . 3. To be ravaged, laid waste, ruined, given up to plunder, Dillī ujaṛ gaī, parjā bigaṛ gaī . (Mutiny of 1857.) 4. To be depopulated; to be without inhabitants. Dillī ujṛī, Shāhjahānābād basā . 5. To be spent, lost, dissipated. Jitnā paisā kamāyā, sab ujaṛ ġayā . 6. To go to waste; to be spent unprofitably; to pass away unenjoyed; to look gloomy, cheerless. Ek pyāre pī binā, sab jag ujṛat jāt . Song. No lower loved, to me the world is naught. 7. To be swept clean out; to be utterly ruined, made wretched etc; to be deprived of one's last penny. Le de-ke ek ṭhīkrā rahā thā, woh bhī ujaṛ gayā . Wom. After paying every body, a tile (house) only was left, and that too is gone. 8. To come to nought; to be all for nothing, Bharā bharāyā ghar ujaṛ gayā . Wom. The whole family has been swept away. 9. To leave the house; to take oneself off; to get out of one's sight. Māe jaīyā, ujaṛ jā! Mah. Wom. Fit to die-hence, out of my sight! 10. To perish; die; become extinct. Jinḍrī ujṛe, dafān ho . Mah. Wom. Die! avaunt! 11. To be violated or dishonored; be deprived of one's virginity. Hāë māmā, ujaṛ gaī maiṅ to! 12. To lose one's husband or wife; to become a widow. Basā basāyā ghar ujaṛ gayā . The light which once cheered the house is extinguished. 13. E. To fall out (the stone of a ring). Is aṅgūṭhī kā nag ujaṛ gayā . E.

v. a. 1. To root up; uproot; plough up. Phr. To pluck up by the roots; to lay the axe to the root. Mūlī ujāṛ-ke, baigan bo de . Rus. 2. To break up; take to pieces. Chhappar ujāṛ-ke banāo . E. 3. To demolish; destroy; pull down; raze to the ground. Sārā shahar ujāṛ-kar mismār kar dȳā . 4. To expel or drive out (the inhabitants); depopulate. Gāoṅ se Kanjar ujāṛ do . Rus. 5. To plunder; rob; carry off. Choroṅ ne sārā khet ujāṛ dȳā . 6. To ruin; bring to poverty; destroy. Is pūt ne merā ghar ujāṛ dȳā . Hin. Wom. 7. To desolate; devastate; spread havoc; ravage. Phr. To ravage with fire and sword; make a clean sweep of. Nādir Shāh ne sārā shahar ūjāṛ kar dȳā . 8. To injure; do harm to; cause hurt. Maiṅ ne terā kyā ujāṛā? What have I done to you? ujā'ṛū , adj. Squandering; wasteful.

ujlī mirćī, White pepper, Piper album

def: s.m. Burning, blazing; shining forth.
s.f. = اجلاپن ujlāpan, q.v. s.m. Brightness, radiance, luminosity; enlightenment; clearness; whiteness; plainness (e.g. us-kā ujlā-pan-hī gahnā thā).
part. adj. (f. -ā) = اجلت ujjalit, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ā), Bright, clear, pure, clean. (See ujlā.)
adj. (dakh.) See اجلا ujlā.
adj. Bright, luminous, shining, radiant, splendid; enlightened; beautiful, lovely, excellent; pure, clean, clear; white; plain, unadorned ( = sāda); simple, guileless; blown, expanded
more: adj. S. ज्वल् to shine. 1. Bright; shining; sparkling; polished. 2. Glassy; crystalline; transparent; clear. 3. White (safed) ; pearly; clean. 1. Ujlo, ujlo, sab bhalo; ujlo bhalo na kes, Nārī nawe, na rip ḍare, na ādar kare nares . Brij. Dohā . All white white things are fair to see, but only not white hair. Nor woman bends, nor foeman fears, nor kings respect white hair. 2. Juār kahe, "maiṅ sab se ujlī," "Jā tan lāgūṅ, wāhī tan khujlī ." Song. Dohā . The juār says, "I am fairest of the fair," "But where I touch, the itch shall break out there." [The natives say that those who live on juār (grain) get itch.] 4. Pure; excellent; praiseworthy. Baṛā ujjal kām kȳā . 5. Enlightened; intelligent; sensible; clever. Woh baṛī ujlī tabiat kā ādmī hui. ujlā ādmī , n. m. 1. A man dressed in clean white clothes (sufaid posh) . 2. A respectable man. 3. A simple man; an intelligent man. ujlā bhaṅgrā …
subs: s.m. A plain or simple man; a man dressed in clean, white clothes; a respectable man

def: s.f. Brightness; cleanness; whiteness, &c. ( = ujlāpan, q.v.); price paid for cleaning, &c.
v.t. = اجالنا ujālnā, q.v. v.t. To make bright; to light, light up, cause to shine; to brighten; to clean, polish, furbish; to make white
more: (caus. of اجالنا) v. a. To cause to cleanse, to brighten.
subs: v.t. To light up, rub up, brighten, polish; to cleanse, make white; to wash (prov.).

def: s.m. Brightness, radiance, luminosity; enlightenment; clearness; whiteness; plainness (e.g. us-kā ujlā-pan-hī gahnā thā).

n. m. Whiteness; brightness; intelligence. ujlā jāmūn, Calyptranthes caryophyllifolia . Wat. A white variety of the fruit. — chan'dan, Santalum album . Wat. — hul'ul, Cleome viscosa . Wat. The viscid cleome , the seeds of which, called chhorī ajwān , are regarded by the native practitioners as anthelmintic and carminative. ujlā dhatūrā, Daturā metel . Wat. — raṅg , A clear, fair, white complexion; transparent color. ujlā kapṛā, ujlī poshāk , Clean, white clothes; decent dress. — kaddū , The long white pumpion or pumpkin, Lagenaria vulgaris . Wat. — kaner, Nerium odorum . Wat. — ghar , n. m. A clean white house; a snug, comfortable house. ujlā mūṅh kar'nā , v. a. To acquit; whitewash; clear one's reputation. ujlā mūṅh ho'nā , v. n. 1. To be whitewashed (one's honor); to clear one's good name. 2. To be acquitted, discharged. uj'lā ho'nā, ujal'nā , v. n. 1. To shine; to be bright, or polished; to become clean. 2. To be clean; to wear clean clothes. ujlī , n. f. Mah. Wom. dhoban . A washerwoman; laundress. ujle pān kī jaṛ, Galangal ; root of Alpinia galanga . Wat. ujlī tulsī , n. f. Ocymum album . Wat. white basil. ujlī kā āzār, Fluo albus ; the whites; leucorrhæa , a discharge of white yellowish or greenish mucus from the vagina . — kāchkūrī, Dolichos pruriens; a variety of cowhage or cowitch, a leguminous plant which produces on the outside of its pods, an irritating substance used in medicine as vermifuge. — guzrān' , A competency, enabling one to afford clean, or white clothes; comfort; high life. — mirch,—mirchī, Piper album; white pepper. uj'jal budh , An enlightened mind; intelligence; good sense (achchī samajh) . Jā kī ujjal budh hai, kāhe lage kusaṅg, Chandan ko bikh nā lage, lipṭe raheṅ bhuaṅg . Song. Dohā . The mind that's pure and good no evil company can soil; The sandal wood's not poisoned though the snake should round it coil.
s.m. Brightness, radiance, luminosity; enlightenment; clearness; whiteness; plainness (e.g. us-kā ujlā-pan-hī gahnā thā).
n. m. Hin. Wom. A present offered to elder women on festive occasions.
incor. for the next, q.v.
more: n. m. ujṛī , n. f. Wom. 1. One who does any thing to destroy or damage the prosperity of her house; a spendthrift; prodigal; wretch. Ujṛe kī shāmat āī hai, ghar luṭāë detā hai . Wom. 2. A bad man; minx; hussy ( nigoṛī ). Ujṛī! ghar chhoṛ-ke kahāṅ ujaṛ gaī thī? Wom. You minx, where have you been wandering out of the house? uj'ṛā puj'ṛā , adj. In ruins; razed to the ground; desolated; ruined. Is se āge baṛhe to dhīṅvar * the, Ujṛe pujṛe unhoṅ ke kuchh ghar the . Mīr.

def: adj. Deserted, desolate, cheerless, gloomy, &c. ( = ujāṛ, q.v.)
subs: adj. In ruins, ruined, razed to the ground, desolate.

part. In ruins, desolate.

r: ʻūju ʼbnu ʻūq yā 'unuq, lit., met.
def: Og, king of Bashan (lit. 'Uj, the son of 'Uq or ʻUnuq); (met.) a tall foolish man.
s.m. A wonderful thing, wonder, miracle, prodigy.

ety: prob. for A. اعجوبة uʻjūbat, v.n. fr. عجب; see ʻajab
def: adj. & s.m. Wonderful ( = ʻajība), a wonderful thing, a wonder; a miracle.
more: (from عجب) adj. Wonderful; (subst.) A wonderful thing.
ex: the seven wonders of the world
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عجوبه ('ujûbâ), probably from Arabic أُعْجُوبَة (ʔuʕjūba).

ety: S. उन्दय˚, caus. of उद् 'to flow'
def: v.t. To cause a person to pour (water, &c.) from one vessel into another; to cause to be emptied; to empty (a vessel, &c. cf. ujhālnā); ujwā-lenā v.t. = ujwanā.
more: E. v. a. 1. To cause one to pour from one vessel into another; to cause to be emptied. 2. To empty (a vessel, house, etc.). E. ( ajvārnā ). 1. Ahe, turte koṭhyā laglaë ajwāre re kī . Mag. Song. Jatāsāṛī . Lo, and behold, in a trice he began to empty the barn. 2. Panhyārā khaṛā hai, pānī ujwā le . Hin. Wom.
v. a. To cause a person to pour from one vessel into another.

def: s.m. = اجالا ujālā, q.v. s.m. Light, day-light, day-break, day; sunshine; splendour, brightness, refulgence; glory, pride, ornament; — adj. (f. -ī), Light, clear, bright, shining, luminous, &c.
subs: To become light; to be lighted, (hence) to kindle up, take fire
s.m. The morning watch, the watch which begins at dawn
s.m. The morning star, the planet Venus.

def: s.f. Omission, slip, mistake, &c. = ćūk, q.v. s.f. Omission, oversight, inadvertency; failing, failure; miss; mistake, blunder, error, fault.
more: n. f. Error; blunder; mistake; slip.

def: (v.n. fr. عقب 'to follow after,' &c.), s.m. & f. An eagle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُقَاب (uqāb), borrowed from Arabic عُقَاب (ʕuqāb).

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻāqil, q.v.), The wise, the prudent.
v.n. To boil up or over; to be boiling, to boil (cf. uklānā).
more: (caus. of اکلنا) v. a. To boil water.

def: v.t. To boil (water, &c.): cf. uklānā.
s.m. The letter उ u
subs: adj. Ending in the letter ukār (उ u).

def: gen. of وه wǒh.
v.n. To be raised; to rise, mount, start up (in, -meṅ); to try to move, struggle to rise, to struggle; to be excited, moved, stirred, roused, &c.
more: E. v. n. 1. To struggle; try to get out, or raise oneself. Kitne to kīchṛoṅ kī daldal meṅ phaṅs rahe haiṅ, Kapṛe tamām gandī daldal meṅ bas rahe haiṅ, Kitne uṭhe haiṅ mar mar, kitne ukas rahe haiṅ, Woh dukh meṅ phaṅs rahe haiṅ, aur log haṅs rahe haiṅ, Kyā kyā machī haiṅ, yāro, barsāt kī bahāreṅ! Barsāt . Nazīr. Here several stuck fast in the mire, All soiled with filth their fine attire, With death-gasps, struggling, some get out, While some are kicking still about, Those flounder in an awful mess, These laugh aloud at their distress. What sport, my friends, the rains bring out! 2. To rise; swell; spring; spout; gush. Sau sau tarah kī chohleṅ, jī meṅ ukastiyāṅ haiṅ, Kyā josh bhar rahe haiṅ, kyā josh mastiyāṅ haiṅ! Holī . Nazīr. A hundred jokes start up and fly about, What ecstasy, what passion fills the rout! 3. To be excited, stirred, roused, stimulated.

v. n. See بهرَکنا (3). 1. To burst forth or leap (a flame); blaze up. 2. To take fire; flare up; to get, fall, or fly into a passion. Phr.—To be flushed with anger, rage, etc.; the eyes flashing fire. 3. To throb; beat; palpitate; quiver. Māthā bhaṛaktā hai . 4. To be dislocated, displaced. 5. To break; crack (crockery). 6. To be over-heated. Tawā bhaṛakne se huqqe kā mazā jātā rahā . 7. To be frightened, scared; to start; shy (a horse). 1. Apne sāë se bhī bhaṛaktī hai, Boṭī boṭī paṛī phaṛaktī hai . Shauq. So delicate, she starts at her own shadow e'en, And every muscle quickening throbs with passion keen. 2. Sipar aur qabze khaṛakne lage, Sawāroṅ ke ghoṛe bhaṛakne lage . Mīr Hasan.
v. n. To be excited, to be moved, to try to move.

def: s. (dakh.) Speech: see اکت ukat.
part. adj. Spoken, said, aforesaid, above-mentioned, the said (used in law documents).

s.f. Speaking, speech, voice, language; story, fabrication, invention, contrivance; inventive power, originality, genius (in these last senses the word is probably derived from the S. udgati; see ugat)
more: Māṛ. adj. Wholly; altogether; quite. Bābū, pahlā kawar jab Sarwan khāë, Akat mahurwā, jaise bujhāë . Mag. Ballad. Sarwan . O Sir, the first mouthful that Sarwan took, The rankest poison seemed, for food mistook.

n. f. Inventive power; genius; originality. Batāë se kȳā, to kyā; apnī ukat se karo, to jāneṅ. ukat se kar'nā , v. a. To do or perform without aid; produce an original work; originate; create; invent.
n. m. An act; law; enactment.
s. f. Speech, speaking. اکت بنانا ukat banānā, To make up a story, to invent, to contrive.
subs: To make up a story, to concoct, fabricate, invent, contrive
To do or perform by means of one's inventive power, to invent, originate, create.

def: v.t. To dig up, turn up, cut up, root up; to unearth; to turn over, turn upside down, upset, turn topsy-turvy; to extract (a secret) gradually and artfully, worm out (a secret); to cast an obligation in one's teeth; to mar, ruin, destroy, spoil, plunder; to rob the good name of, to vilify, abuse; — v. int. To be dug up, turned, cut up, &c.: ukaṭ-ḍālna, v.t. (intens. of ukaṭnā, — generally used in the sense of), to tear, tear to pieces, root up, utterly ruin or destroy, &c.
more: (perhaps, उत् and r. कड Break, separate) v. a. To dig up slowly a thing buried in the ground, to extract a secret gradually and artfully, to pump.

def: v. a. S. कड्, H. kor, kār to dig, take out. 1. To dig up; root up ( ukhāṛnā ). 2. To extract (a secret) artfully; to pump; worm out. 3. To turn over, or upside down; upset; rummage. 4. To remind one of an obligation. 5. To abuse; revile; call names; insult. 1. Lāī dugānā uf kā na kalmah zabān par, Ukṭī tumhāre bhaīyā ne us kī falān tak . Wom. Jān Sāhab. No word nor sound at all escaped my twin-life's lips, Foul names your brother called her—e'en that between the hips. 2. Jo mujh ko kahogī, to maiṅ tumhāre kunbe ko ukṭūṅgī . Wom. If you say a word to me, I'll abuse you and all your kith and kin. 6. To carry off; rob; spoil; plunder. Sārā khet ukaṭ ḍālā . Rus.
v.t. To cause to be dug up, &c.
s.f. (prov.) The longing of a pregnant woman.
s.m. End, conclusion, termination; last state; accomplishment; consequence, result, issue; the future state, the next world (syn. ʻāqibat).
more: (from عقب) s. m. End, conclusion, accomplishment, futurity, future state.

ety: for A. عقدة, v.n. fr. عقد 'to tie'
def: s.m. A knot, tie, bond ( = ʻaqd); a knob; a joint; a node; an excrescence; a gland; an impediment (in speech); an entanglement, a complication; entangled things, perplexed affairs, confused words; a knotty problem; a secret, mystery, enigma
more: n. m. 1. A hard knot or problem. 2. An excrescence; a node. 3. A secret; mystery; enigma. Āj unkā bhī uqdā khul gayā . 4. Entanglement ( uljhāo , 2). uqda-kushāī , n. f. The untying or opening of knots or difficulties.

(from عقد) s. m. 1. A knot, an excrescence (especially, at the joints, or where bones have been broken), a node. 2. An impediment in speech. 3. Perplexed affairs, entangled things, confused words. عقده کشا uﻌḳda-kushā, Resolver of perplexities or difficulties. عقده کساٴي uﻌḳda-kushā-ī, s. f. The untying or opening of knots or difficulties.
subs: s.m. 'An opener of knots,' one who solves difficulties, &c.
s.f. The opening or untying of knots, the solving of difficulties.

def: v.t. To turn up, to undo, unpick, unravel, open out, rip up, &c.
s.f. (in H. also m.), Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum (in N.W. India generally called īkh)
subs: ūkh-bhoj, s.m. The day on which the planting of the sugar-cane begins (it is generally attended with some festive ceremonies)

def: Māṛ., n. f. S. इक्ष Pr. uchhchū . Sugarcane; Saccharum officinarum . Wat. 1. Tulsī, jag meṅ āë-ke, sīkh ūkh kī leo, Jā tum ko anrath kare, wā ko ras tum deo. Dohā . Tulsī Dās. O Tulsi, from the sugarcane do thou a lesson learn, Who bruiseth thee do thou to him thy life's sap give in turn. 2. Māgh kā jāṛā, Jeṭh kī dhūp, Baṛe kashṭ se upje ūkh . Prov. With frost in February, heat in June, The sugarcane with difficulty's grown.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit इक्षु (ikṣu).

def: s.m. Throwing up, vomiting; vomit; an emetic
more: n. The act of vomiting or throwing up; an emetic. ukhāl' pukhāl',—wakhāl ,' Rus. n. m. Vomiting; cholera morbus.

def: s.f. A wooden or stone mortar for pounding rice or other grain.
s.f. A mortar to pound grain in = ukhli, q.v. s.f. = H اکهل उखल ukhal (contrac. of ūkhal),s.m. A wooden or stone mortar for pounding rice or other grain.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀮 (ukkhala), from Sanskrit *उदुक्खल (*udukkhala), per Turner.

def: v. a. (dakh.) To vomit ( Harris ).
v.t. To throw up, vomit
more: v. a. (dakh.) To vomit ( Harris ): see اوکهالنا ūkhālnā.

def: s.m. Heat, &c., see ushma.
s.f. A pot or vessel for husking grain; — a small mortar = okhlī, q.v.
n. f. S. उत् up, खड् to break off. Rooting up; extirpation; eradication. ukhāṛ pachhāṛ' , n. f. Backbiting; evil-speaking; slander. Tumhārī ukhāṛ pachhāṛ se merī naukrī gaī .
s.f. Rooting up, extirpation, eradication
more: (see اکهاڙنا) s. f. Rooting up, extirpation.

def: s.f. A field of sugar-cane.
more: n. f. A field of sugarcane.

def: s.f. A field of sugar-cane.

ety: ukhāṛ˚ = Prk. उक्कड्ढ(इ), fr. उक्कड्ढ = S. उत्कृष्ट, p.p.p. of उद्+कृष्
def: v.t. To root up, eradicate, extirpate, pluck up or out, tear up or off, dig up; to break off, disjoin, sunder; to dislocate, displace; to take out, extract, draw out; to detach, separate, remove; to set (one) against, estrange, alienate; entice away, win over (from), buy off; to drive away, put away, do away with, dismiss; to upset, overturn, subvert; to scatter, disperse; to demolish, desolate, destroy, lay waste, devastate, exterminate
more: (trans. of اکهڙنا q. v.) v. a. To root up, eradicate, extirpate, to break or pluck up, to dislocate.

def: v.n. To be rooted up or out, be plucked up or out, be extracted, be dug up, be extirpated; to be wiped out, effaced; to be scattered, dispersed; to be pulled down, razed, demolished, &c.; to break up; to be struck (a tent); to slip out, be dislocated (a bone); to be pulled, torn or ripped off; to be broken or severed (as friendship); to become alienated or estranged; to become displeased or offended (with, -se); to be seared, frightened, driven away, &c. to be discouraged, disheartened, turned from, diverted, dissuaded, &c.; to make off, decamp, abscond, slink off, &c.; to be dismissed, sent adrift, &c.; to come undone, unravelled, unstitched, &c.; to come out, fall off or out (as the stone of a ring); to fall of, decline; to lose credit or reputation; to fail of (one's word); to sing or play out of tune; to be unsteady, shaky, straggling, irregular, inconsistent, &c. (see ukhṛā); to be cut, carved, or engraved (on, -par); to be stamped, impressed, &c.
more: v. n. To be rooted up, plucked up or raised up, to slip out (as a bone, &c.). اکهڙنا پکهڙنا ukhaṛna pukhaṛnā, To be plucked or rooted up.
subs: v.n. intens. of ukhaṛnā, and the more common form.

def: v. a. See اکهاڙنا ukhāṛnā.

v. a. 1. To tear out; pluck out; pluck up; grub up; rake up; root up; root out ( upāṛnā ); extirpate; eradicate. Jaṛ se ukhāṛnā . To pull up by the roots. 2. To sunder; disjoin; set free; unloose; unfix. 3. To take out; extract; pluck; draw out. Kīl ukhāṛ lo . 4. To put out of place; displace; dislocate. Bachche kī bāṅ ukhāṛ ḍālī . 5. To detach; separate; part; break; take out, or off; remove. Chippī ukhāṛ ḍālī . E. 6. To alienate; estrange; put against; poison (one's mind) ( bis bonā ). 7. To put away; remove; clear the way; sweep away; do away with; dismiss ( nikāl denā ); drive away. 8. To upset; overturn; subvert. 9. To demolish; destroy; pull down; raze to the ground; lay waste; devastate; desolate ( ujāṛnā ); put an end to; exterminate. 1. Khaṛī khetī ukhāṛ ḍālī . 2. Bāgyoṅ ke ghar ukheṛ-ke pheṅk dȳë . 10. To disperse; scatter; put to flight; cause a panic. 1. Āṅdhī ne jamā jamāyā melā ukhāṛ dȳā . 2. Bārā-panī top ne fauj ke pāoṅ ukhāṛ dȳë . 11. To fan; rake up; renew, or revive old quarrels. Gaṛe murde ukheṛnā . To dig up the buried dead. ukhāṛ lenā , v. a. To bring over; buy off; entice away. kān ukhāṛnā,—maṛoṛnā , v. a. To twist, or pull one's ears; to punish.
s.m. One who roots or digs up, eradicator, devastator, destroyer, &c.
n. m. One who digs up, or uproots.
v.t. To cause to be rooted out, to have (a thing) pulled out, &c.
v.n. To trip, stumble.
v.t. = اکهيڙنا ukheṛnā, q.v. v.t. = اکهاڙنا ukhāṛnā, q.v.
subs: v.n. To be crusty or cross, to turn away the face (from), to be cool (towards).
